methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Aepnus Technology is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Lukas Hackl and Bilen Akuzum that has raised $10.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private frontier-tech companies in the climate and energy sector, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $10.6M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $26M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector companies at Series A stage—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yielding a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M to $200M. These companies represent the immediate competitive and funding-stage cohort most relevant for benchmarking.

The triangulated analysis across both methods produces a valuation range of $23M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $88M. The wide dispersion reflects tension between stage-appropriate valuation anchored to capital deployed and peer multiples at a later funding stage. Key uncertainties include the applicability of Series A peer valuations to a seed-stage company and the degree to which Aepnus's technology differentiation or market timing justify positioning toward the upper or lower bound of the range.
